# Software Engineering Best Practices

## Bold Text

The most important rule in software development is **never deploy on a Friday**. Some teams use __double underscores__ for emphasis in their documentation guidelines.

**This entire paragraph is bold.** It demonstrates that marks can wrap a full block of inline content without breaking the parser.

## Italic Text

When we talk about *separation of concerns*, we mean that each module should handle one responsibility. Some older codebases use _underscores_ for italic instead of asterisks.

*This entire paragraph is italic.* It serves as a stress test for paragraph-level marks.

## Inline Code

Use the `git rebase` command carefully. The configuration lives in `~/.config/app/settings.json` and should not be edited manually.

To check the status, run `docker ps --filter "status=running"` and verify the output.

Inline code with backticks inside: ``the `backtick` character is used for code``.

Inline code containing asterisks: `**this is not bold**` and `*this is not italic*`.

## Strikethrough

The old API endpoint was ~~`/api/v1/users`~~ and has been replaced with the new versioned path. We also removed ~~the legacy authentication flow~~ in favor of OAuth 2.0.

## Links

Read the [official documentation](https://example.com/docs) for more details. The team also maintains a [style guide](https://example.com/style "Internal Style Guide") with additional conventions.

For quick access, visit <https://example.com/dashboard>.

## Nested and Combined Marks

When reviewing code, pay attention to ***critically important*** patterns that could affect performance.

Use **`npm run build`** to compile the project before deploying.

Check the [*italicized link text*](https://example.com) for the updated policy.

The function ~~`deprecated_handler()`~~ was removed in **version 3.2**.

We recommend using ***`bold italic code`*** sparingly in documentation.

## Adjacent Marks

**Bold text** followed by *italic text* and then `inline code` and ~~strikethrough~~ all in the same paragraph, separated by regular text.

Start of paragraph **bold at the start**. And now *italic at the end*.

## Edge Cases

A single **word** bolded. A single *word* italicized. A single `word` in code.

**Two words** bolded together. Multiple **separate** bold **segments** in one line.

A link with complex URL: [search results](https://example.com/search?q=hello+world&lang=en#results).
